stemHtml: When roll spoilers are extended, the part of the wing on which they are mounted:

options.a: experiences a reduction in lift, which generates the desired rolling moment. In addition there is a local increase in drag, which suppresses adverse yaw.
options.b: experiences extra drag, which generates a yawing moment. The speed difference between both wings generates the desired rolling moment.
options.c: is forced downwards as a reaction to the increased drag.
options.d: stalls. This causes a difference in lift between both wings, which generates the desired rolling moment.
